nodes.label: feat: initial auth module with models and service

metadata.author: Sumit
metadata.msg:
feat: initial auth module with models and service

- models.py: in-memory user store with create_user and get_user
- service.py: signup and login with basic validation
- no hashing, no persistence -- intentionally dumb for iteration

feat: tree-sitter ingestion, HelixDB writes, semantic pass,

metadata.author: Sumit
metadata.msg:
feat: tree-sitter ingestion, HelixDB writes, semantic pass, viz dump

- scalable_ingest.py: replaced ast with tree-sitter; extracts CONTAINS,
  IMPORTS, INHERITS, CALLS edges; writes all nodes/edges live to HelixDB
  (localhost:6969) via helix-db SDK; keeps graph_payload.json + graph_viz.json
  as artefacts

- semantic_pass.py: separate post-ingestion pass; traverses Commit->GENERATED->
  FunctionState in HelixDB; analyses code structurally (params, calls, branches,
  returns) to derive per-function Concept nodes and a ChangeNode summary;
  attaches Rationale from commit message body; guardrail prevents writing
  AST-owned node kinds

- dump_viz.py: merges graph_payload.json with semantic nodes from HelixDB into
  graph_viz.json for tools/harness_graph_viz.html

- tools/harness_graph_viz.html: force-directed graph explorer (load JSON)

nodes.label: fix: cross-file CALLS resolution, filter stdlib noise, fix s

metadata.author: Sumit
metadata.msg:
fix: cross-file CALLS resolution, filter stdlib noise, fix semantic edges in viz

- scalable_ingest: func_registry shared across files per commit; CALLS edges
  only emitted for known user-defined functions (filters out get/append/print etc)
- dump_viz: replaced broken zip-based edge approach with per-node traversal
  using parameterized HelixDB queries; semantic edges now correctly appear

nodes.label: fix: skip tooling files from ingestion, add NEXT_COMMIT time

metadata.author: Sumit
metadata.msg:
fix: skip tooling files from ingestion, add NEXT_COMMIT timeline chain

- scalable_ingest: _SKIP_FILES list excludes ingestion scripts from being
  parsed as code nodes; NEXT_COMMIT edges link commits in timeline order
- dump_viz: includes NEXT_COMMIT in semantic edge traversal

nodes.label: feat: add validation, error handling, and account management

metadata.author: Sumit
metadata.msg:
feat: add validation, error handling, and account management to auth module

- models: add user_exists, delete_user, list_users with validation
- service: add custom exceptions (AuthError, ValidationError, AuthenticationError)
- service: add logout and delete_account functions
- service: return structured JSON responses instead of plain strings
- service: validate username format (alphanumeric, min 3 chars)
- models: enforce password min length (6 chars)

nodes.label: feat: agent tooling — status tracking, vector index, MCP ser

metadata.author: Sumit
metadata.msg:
feat: agent tooling — status tracking, vector index, MCP server, hand-written semantic summaries

- scalable_ingest: add status='active'/'superseded' on FunctionState nodes,
  create vector index on FunctionState.ai_summary
- semantic_pass: replace AST-only analysis with hand-written summaries for all
  auth module functions; LLM hook stub left for future wiring
- tools/graph_tools.py: 4 DSL-based agent tools (search_code_semantics,
  get_code_time_travel_diff, trace_blast_radius, get_temporal_vulnerability_trace)
- tools/graph_mcp_server.py: HTTP MCP server exposing tools on port 7700
- .kiro/settings/mcp.json: registers graphdb MCP server with Kiro

nodes.label: fix: resolve all traversal bugs — set_property patch, multi-

metadata.author: Sumit
metadata.msg:
fix: resolve all traversal bugs — set_property patch, multi-hop Python join, GENERATED query

- semantic_pass: replace drop+reinsert with set_property (edges no longer destroyed)
- semantic_pass: fix _get_states_for_commit — drop NodeRef.var chaining, use direct
  chained traversal g().n_with_label(Commit).out(GENERATED) which HelixDB resolves correctly
- scalable_ingest: add graph_mcp_server.py + graph_tools.py to _SKIP_FILES
- graph_tools: refactor get_temporal_vulnerability_trace into 3-hop Python join
  (hop1: find callers, hop2a: caller->HAS_STATE, hop2b: state<-GENERATED with ts filter)
  avoids mixed-direction single-batch limitation

All 4 tools now validated:
  trace_blast_radius: signup correctly identified as caller of create_user
  get_temporal_vulnerability_trace: returns commit_623241a (pre-upgrade stale caller)
  get_code_time_travel_diff: signup eef5695 current + 623241a previous with full code+summary
  search_code_semantics: login + delete_account returned for auth query

nodes.label: feat: edit_code tool — closes the agentic loop (trace -> dif

metadata.author: Sumit
metadata.msg:
feat: edit_code tool — closes the agentic loop (trace -> diff -> patch -> re-ingest)

- graph_tools: add edit_code(file, function_name, new_code) — AST-based function
  replacement in working tree; re-runs scalable_ingest after write
- graph_mcp_server: register edit_code tool in manifest + TOOL_MAP
- auth/service.py: patched by agent via edit_code — signup now enforces 8-char
  minimum password (was 6 in models, now enforced at service layer too)
- tools/_test_loop.py: end-to-end test script for the full agentic loop

Validated loop: get_temporal_vulnerability_trace identified stale signup@623241a,
get_code_time_travel_diff showed old vs new behavior, edit_code patched auth/service.py
lines 30-42 and re-ingested successfully
